bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Single-Cell & Spatial Omics

Computational methods for scRNA-seq, ATAC-seq, trajectory inference, cell-cell communication, and spatial transcriptomics.

20 papers curated · High: 4 · Medium: 16 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A deep-learning-informed prior and Bayesian model for differential AP-MS interactome analysis

This paper presents BayesInteractomics, a new computational framework that combines deep learning with Bayesian statistics to improve the analysis of affinity-purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) data for mapping protein-protein interactions. By using a neural network trained on protein structures to predict direct binding and incorporating this as prior knowledge, the method outperforms existing tools like SAINTexpress and CompPASS in distinguishing genuine interactors from background noise.

proteomicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

scJET: Full-gene Space Single-cell Expression Generation with Patch-based Transformer Modeling

scJET is a new generative model that uses a patch-based Transformer to generate full-gene expression matrices from single-cell RNA-seq data, overcoming the limitations of previous methods that only work with highly variable genes or low-dimensional representations. It preserves both global and local data structure as well as gene-level expression programs, making it a more comprehensive tool for transcriptome-wide analysis.

single-celltranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

SPECTER-Based Semantic Triage of Biomedical Literature for Systematic Reviews in Mutational Signature Analysis

This paper presents a new pipeline that uses SPECTER scientific-document embeddings combined with research-question similarity and keyword coverage to automatically rank studies for systematic reviews in mutational signature analysis. The method was tested on over 2,200 biomedical records and shows improved performance over traditional keyword-only and TF-IDF approaches for screening relevant literature.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BBBP_Atlas: Unified Interpretable Modeling of Blood Brain Barrier Permeability across Small Molecules and Peptides

The authors present BBBP_Atlas, a unified and interpretable model for predicting blood-brain barrier permeability that works across both small molecules and peptides, overcoming limitations of previous models that were restricted to small molecules or required predefined descriptors. They also address data fragmentation and leakage issues by standardizing publicly available BBBP datasets.

drug-discoverymachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mutational bias shapes protein evolution across RNA viruses

This study shows that mutational biases, not just natural selection, play a major role in shaping protein evolution in RNA viruses. Using SARS-CoV-2 as a model, the authors found that the virus's mutational spectrum explains over 50% of observed amino acid substitutions and predicts changes in protein composition during the pandemic.

evolutiongenomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Metabolic Rewiring in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Systems Analysis of TCGA-BRCA Transcriptome Reveals Prognostic Hub Genes

This study uses a systems biology approach to analyze transcriptomic data from TNBC patients, identifying key metabolic hub genes that are rewired in this aggressive cancer subtype. The authors pinpoint ATP1A2 as a significant prognostic marker, suggesting it could be a potential therapeutic target. The work provides a network-level view of metabolic adaptations in TNBC, offering new insights into the disease's biology.

transcriptomicssystems-biologycancer
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Learning the spatial cell-cell communication network to decode multi-channel signaling and predict network-hub vulnerabilities with MOSANIC

MOSANIC is a new computational method that learns spatial cell-cell communication networks from multi-channel imaging data, enabling the identification of signaling hubs and prediction of network vulnerabilities. It allows researchers to analyze how signals propagate through tissues and predict the effects of losing specific cell types on overall tissue communication.

spatial-omicsnetwork-analysistool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Assessing tensor decomposition quality of immune profiling data from a dictionary learning perspective

This paper presents new metrics for assessing the quality of non-negative tensor decompositions applied to immune profiling data, helping researchers choose the optimal rank and trial. By framing the problem from a dictionary learning perspective, the authors show how considering the underlying experimental data model can improve decomposition quality assessment.

methodologymachine-learningimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Generative AI Models Reveal Dynamic Views of Aging (DyViA) Phenotypes in Healthy Individuals

DyViA is a generative AI platform that uses diffusion and GAN models to create personalized aging trajectories for healthy individuals, predicting how specific phenotypes change with age. The tool offers a new way to model healthy aging dynamics at an individual level.

machine-learningdeep-learningaging
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A five-dimensional functional state space for fingerprinting disease transcriptomes

This paper introduces a five-dimensional functional state space framework that maps disease transcriptomes into coordinated activity patterns across major biological systems, enabling easier comparison across conditions. The approach provides a reproducible pipeline for functional scoring and cross-condition analysis, moving beyond fragmented gene and pathway lists.

transcriptomicsmethodologysystems-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization for library size in bulk RNA-seq

This paper identifies that standard library size normalization methods for bulk RNA-seq often fail to fully remove correlation between gene expression counts and sequencing depth, especially in large datasets. The authors propose a gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization approach that more effectively eliminates this technical artifact, improving the reliability of differential expression analyses.

transcriptomicsmethodologytool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

IgGM2: An All-Atom Foundation Model for Adaptive Immune Receptor Design

IgGM2 is a new AI model that can simultaneously design the sequence and 3D structure of immune receptors like antibodies and T-cell receptors, which is more accurate than previous methods that handled these tasks separately. It uses an all-atom generative approach to model how sequence, shape, and target binding work together across different receptor types.

machine-learningprotein-structureimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Gene Program Negotiation Defines Cellular Identity in Single-Cell Transcriptomes

This paper introduces Gene Program Negotiation (GPN), a graph-based computational framework that models how multiple simultaneously active gene programs collectively determine cellular identity in single-cell transcriptomes, overcoming the limitations of existing methods that analyze programs independently. By reconstructing cell-specific program interactions, GPN provides deeper insight into regulatory decision-making and cellular heterogeneity.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Rectangle: robust and scalable multiscale deconvolution informed by single-cell RNA sequencing data

Rectangle is a new Python framework for deconvolving bulk RNA-seq data using single-cell references. It improves resolution of closely related cell types, scales efficiently to large datasets, and accounts for cell types not present in the reference.

transcriptomicssingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mind the Alignment Gap: A Spatial Transcriptomics Benchmark for Scientific Coding Agents

This paper introduces a framework for automatically building benchmarks to evaluate scientific coding agents, using spatial transcriptomics alignment as a case study. The authors constructed 40 tasks from peer-reviewed papers, where agents must produce coordinate tables for alignment, and they provide tools for diagnosing agent behavior through trace inspection. This work offers a path to accelerating the evaluation of AI agents in scientific research by automating benchmark construction.

spatial-omicstoolmachine-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Coding agents author interpretable single-cell embedding models from the literature

This paper introduces a method where coding agents automatically generate interpretable single-cell embedding models by extracting marker-gene programs from the literature, rather than learning dense dimensions de novo. This approach produces sparse, named dimensions that are inherently interpretable and require less post-hoc analysis.

single-cellmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

LeafRank: A phylodynamic framework for inferring relative fitness from single-cell phylogenies in chromosomally unstable tumors

LeafRank is a new computational method that uses single-cell DNA sequencing data to infer the relative fitness of individual cancer cells in tumors with chromosomal instability. By modeling tumor evolution as a branching process, it can estimate which cells are more likely to drive tumor growth, even in complex scenarios like whole-genome duplication.

cancersingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

EZSolver: Template-free prediction of polar enzymatic mechanisms via bidirectional flow matching and search

EZSolver is a new computational tool that predicts how enzymes catalyze reactions without relying on predefined rules, using a generative AI approach called flow matching. It outperforms existing methods on novel chemical reactions, offering a more flexible way to understand and engineer enzymes.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BertST: BERT-based Spatial Domain Identification in Patient Data

This paper introduces BertST, a BERT-based method for identifying spatial domains in spatial transcriptomics data. By adapting the BERT architecture to jointly model local and long-range dependencies in gene expression and spatial information, BertST improves the accuracy and coherence of tissue region partitioning compared to existing graph neural network approaches.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Thematic Shifts in Early-High-Impact Cancer Genomics and Diagnostics Research: A Bibliometric and Semantic Analysis

This study uses bibliometric analysis and large language models to identify which topics in cancer genomics and diagnostics research gain early citation prominence. By analyzing over 10,000 articles per year, the authors reveal thematic shifts that can guide research investment and clinical translation.

cancermachine-learningmethodology
